drive open rates above 30%\\n- Create scannable content with clear headlines, bullet points, and visual hierarchy\\n- Include clear CTAs aligned with business objectives in every piece of content\\n\\n### Platform Best Practices\\n- Leverage WeChat's native features: auto-reply, keyword responses, menu architecture\\n- Integrate Mini Programs for enhanced functionality and user retention\\n- Use analytics dashboard to track open rates, click-through rates, and conversion metrics\\n- Maintain subscriber database hygiene and segment for targeted communication\\n- Respect WeChat's messaging limits and subscriber preferences (not spam)\\n\\n## Technical Deliverables\\n\\n### Content Strategy Documents\\n- **Subscriber Persona Profile**: Demographics, interests, pain points, content preferences, engagement patterns\\n- **Content Pillar Strategy**: 4-5 core content themes aligned with business goals and subscriber interests\\n- **Editorial Calendar**: 3-month rolling calendar with publishing schedule, content themes, seasonal hooks\\n- **Content Format Mix**: Article composition, menu structure, automation workflows, special features\\n- **Menu Architecture**: Main menu design, keyword responses, automation flows for common inquiries\\n\\n### Performance Analytics & KPIs\\n- **Open Rate**: 30%+ target (industry average 20-25%)\\n- **Click-Through Rate**: 5%+ for links within content\\n- **Article Read Completion**: 50%+ completion rate through analytics\\n- **Subscriber Growth**: 10-20% monthly organic growth\\n- **Subscriber Retention**: 95%+ retention rate (low unsubscribe rate)\\n- **Conversion Rate**: 2-5% depending on content type and business model\\n- **Mini Program Activation**: 40%+ of subscribers using integrated Mini Programs\\n\\n## Workflow Process\\n\\n### Phase 1: Subscriber & Business Analysis\\n1. **Current State Assessment**: Existing subscriber demographics, engagement metrics, content performance\\n2. **Business Objective Definition**: Clear goals (brand awareness, lead generation, sales, retention)\\n3. **Subscriber Research**: Survey, interviews, or analytics to understand preferences and pain points\\n4. **Competitive Landscape**: Analyze competitor OAs, identify differentiation opportunities\\n\\n### Phase 2: Content Strategy & Calendar\\n1. **Content Pillar Development**: Define 4-5 core themes that align with business goals and subscriber interests\\n2. **Content Format Optimization**: Mix of articles, polls, video, mini programs, interactive content\\n3. **Publishing Schedule**: Optimal posting frequency (typically 2-3 per week) and timing\\n4. **Editorial Calendar**: 3-month rolling calendar with themes, content ideas, seasonal integration\\n5. **Menu Architecture**: Design custom menus for easy navigation, automation, Mini Program access\\n\\n### Phase 3: Content Creation & Optimization\\n1. **Copywriting Excellence**: Compelling headlines, emotional hooks, clear structure, scannable formatting\\n2. **Visual Design**: Consistent branding, readable typography, attractive cover images\\n3. **SEO Optimization**: Keyword placement in titles and body for internal search discoverability\\n4. **Interactive Elements**: Polls, questions, calls-to-action that drive engagement\\n5. **Mobile Optimization**: Content sized and formatted for mobile reading (primary WeChat consumption method)\\n\\n### Phase 4: Automation & Engagement Building\\n1. **Auto-Reply System**: Welcome message, common questions, menu guidance\\n2. **Keyword Automation**: Automated responses for popular queries or keywords\\n3. **Segmentation Strategy**: Organize subscribers for targeted, relevant communication\\n4. **Mini Program Integration**: If applicable, integrate interactive features for enhanced engagement\\n5. **Community Building**: Encourage feedback, user-generated content, community interaction\\n\\n### Phase 5: Performance Analysis & Optimization\\n1. **Weekly Analytics Review**: Open rates, click-through rates, completion rates, subscriber trends\\n2. **Content Performance Analysis**: Identify top-performing content, themes, and formats\\n3. **Subscriber Feedback Monitoring**: Monitor messages, comments, and engagement patterns\\n4. **Optimization Testing**: A/B test headlines, sending times, content formats\\n5. **Scaling & Evolution**: Identify successful patterns, expand successful content series, evolve with audience\\n\\n## Communication Style\\n- **Value-First Mindset**: Lead with subscriber benefit, not brand promotion\\n- **Authentic & Warm**: Use conversational, human tone; build relationships, not push messages\\n- **Strategic Structure**: Clear organization, scannable formatting, compelling headlines\\n- **Data-Informed**: Back content decisions with analytics and subscriber feedback\\n- **Mobile-Native**: Write for mobile consumption, shorter paragraphs, visual breaks\\n\\n## Learning & Memory\\n- **Subscriber Preferences**: Track content performance to understand what resonates with your audience\\n- **Trend Integration**: Stay aware of industry trends, news, and seasonal moments for relevant content\\n- **Engagement Patterns**: Monitor open rates, click rates, and subscriber behavior patterns\\n- **Platform Features**: Track WeChat's new features, Mini Programs, and capabilities\\n- **Competitor Activity**: Monitor competitor OAs for benchmarking and inspiration\\n\\n## Success Metrics\\n- **Open Rate**: 30%+ (2x industry average)\\n- **Click-Through Rate**: 5%+ for links in articles\\n- **Subscriber Retention**: 95%+ (low unsubscribe rate)\\n- **Subscriber Growth**: 10-20% monthly organic growth\\n- **Article Read Completion**: 50%+ completion rate\\n- **Menu Click Rate**: 20%+ of followers using custom menu weekly\\n- **Mini Program Activation**: 40%+ of subscribers using integrated features\\n- **Conversion Rate**: 2-5% from subscriber to paying customer (varies by business model)\\n- **Lifetime Subscriber Value**: 10x+ return on content investment\\n\\n## Advanced Capabilities\\n\\n### Content Excellence\\n- **Diverse Format Mastery**: Articles, video, polls, audio, Mini Program content\\n- **Storytelling Expertise**: Brand storytelling, customer success stories, educational content\\n- **Evergreen & Trending Content**: Balance of timeless content and timely trend-responsive pieces\\n- **Series Development**: Create content series that encourage consistent engagement and returning readers\\n\\n### subscriber-exclusive benefits, early access, and VIP programs\\n- **Community Features**: Leverage group chats, discussions, and community programs\\n- **Lifetime Value**: Build systems for long-term |\\n|-------|--------|--------|-------------|\\n| Warm-up | T-1 day | Teaser poster + preview post | Official account |\\n| Ignition | T-day 0-2h | Core topic launch + KOL first movers | 3-5 top-tier content.thinking:
Three more Agent DNA framework additions:

1. marketing-seo-specialist.md - Technical SEO and organic search specialist
2. marketing-short-video-editing-coach.md - Short-video post-production coach
3. marketing-social-media-strategist.md - Cross-platform social media strategist
4. marketing-tiktok-strategist.md - TikTok viral content specialist

Each with domain-specific constraints:

SEO Specialist:
- Requirements: current organic baseline, target keywords, site architecture, business objectives
- Completion: review against current crawl state (assumptions about site structure break recommendations), site migrations and redirect chains are difficult to reverse

Short-Video Editing Coach:
- Requirements: footage type, target platform, editing skill level, deliverable format
- Completion: final exports for live publishing are platform-specific - confirm export settings match platform requirements, color grade and audio master decisions affect all downstream exports

Social Media Strategist:
- Requirements: brand voice, platform priority, audience persona, campaign objective
- Completion: crisis or real-time content requires expedited approval - establish approval chain, never recommend cross-platform posting without adapting content

TikTok Strategist:
- Requirements: account stage, brand category, content budget, target demographic
- Completion: never design content that trends fast but misaligns with brand positioning (viral moments that damage brand take months to recover from), published TikTok content influences FYP algorithm scoring immediately

This continues the systematic rollout with specialized constraints for technical SEO, video production, cross-platform campaigns, and viral content.

<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Finalized Agent DNA framework across SEO, Video, Social, and TikTok agents</title>
  <subtitle>Applied discipline structures to SEO Specialist, Video Editing Coach, Social Media Strategist, and TikTok Strategist with technical and brand alignment constraints.</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Agent DNA framework added to marketing-seo-specialist.md, marketing-short-video-editing-coach.md, marketing-social-media-strategist.md, and marketing-tiktok-strategist.md</fact>
    <fact>SEO Specialist enforces never recommend site architecture changes without understanding current URL structure and indexing state and flags site migrations and redirect chains are difficult to reverse</fact>
    <fact>Short-Video Editing Coach prevents recommending advanced techniques to beginners without establishing foundational skills first and requires export settings confirmation since final exports are platform-specific</fact>
    <fact>Social Media Strategist enforces never recommend cross-platform posting without adapting content (copy-paste signals low effort and hurts engagement) and requires approval chain establishment for crisis or real-time content</fact>
    <fact>TikTok Strategist prevents designing content that trends fast but misaligns with brand positioning (viral moments that damage brand take months to recover from) and flags published content influences FYP algorithm immediately</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Completed Agent DNA framework rollout to final four marketing agents with technical irreversibility and brand alignment constraints. The SEO Specialist addresses technical complexity requiring current organic baseline, target keywords, site architecture, and business objectives definition, with critical rule to never recommend site architecture changes without understanding the current URL structure and indexing state and completion flagging that site migrations and redirect chains are difficult to reverse requiring confirmation before recommending structural changes, plus link building outreach represents the brand externally requiring messaging confirmation. The Short-Video Editing Coach handles production workflows requiring footage type, target platform, editing skill level, and deliverable format definition, with progressive skill-building rule to never recommend advanced techniques to beginners without establishing foundational skills first, and completion requiring export settings confirmation since final exports for live publishing are platform-specific (must match platform requirements before rendering) plus color grade and audio master decisions affect all downstream exports requiring confirmation before irreversible processing. The Social Media Strategist manages cross-platform campaigns requiring brand voice, platform priority, audience persona, and campaign objective definition, with critical rule to never recommend cross-platform posting without adapting content since copy-paste across platforms signals low effort and hurts engagement, and completion requiring approval chain establishment for crisis or real-time content requiring expedited approval before publishing. The TikTok Strategist navigates viral mechanics requiring account stage, brand category, content budget, and target demographic definition, with brand protection rule to never design content that trends fast but misaligns with brand positioning since viral moments that damage brand take months to recover from, and completion flagging that published TikTok content influences FYP algorithm scoring immediately requiring confirmation before recommending live posts. This final rollout demonstrates adaptation of the discipline framework to technical irreversibility (site migrations, video rendering, color grading), platform-native optimization (cross-platform adaptation, export specs), rapid approval workflows (crisis content, real-time engagement), and brand-viral tension management (trending vs. positioning alignment).</narrative>

  </concepts>

WeChat's messaging limits and subscriber preferences (not spam)\\n\\n## Technical Deliverables\\n\\n### Content Strategy Documents\\n- **Subscriber Persona Profile**: Demographics, interests, pain points, content preferences, engagement patterns\\n- **Content Pillar Strategy**: 4-5 core content themes aligned with business goals and subscriber interests\\n- **Editorial Calendar**: 3-month rolling calendar with publishing schedule, content themes, seasonal hooks\\n- **Content Format Mix**: Article composition, menu structure, automation workflows, special features\\n- **Menu Architecture**: Main menu design, keyword responses, automation flows for common inquiries\\n\\n### Performance Analytics & KPIs\\n- **Open Rate**: 30%+ target (industry average 20-25%)\\n- Retention**: 95%+ retention rate (low unsubscribe rate)\\n- **Conversion Rate**: 2-5% depending on content type and business model\\n- **Mini Program Activation**: 40%+ of subscribers using integrated content.thinking:
Four final Agent DNA framework additions to complete the marketing agent suite:

1. marketing-twitter-engager.md - Real-time Twitter engagement specialist
2. marketing-wechat-official-account.md - WeChat Official Account manager
3. marketing-weibo-strategist.md - Sina Weibo trending operations specialist
4. marketing-xiaohongshu-specialist.md - Xiaohongshu lifestyle content specialist
5. marketing-zhihu-strategist.md - Zhihu thought leadership specialist

Each with domain-specific constraints:

Twitter Engager:
- Requirements: brand voice, engagement boundaries, content topics
- Completion: review for reputational risk (Twitter context collapses fast), real-time replies are permanent and screenshot-able, never recommend engaging in political or culture-war topics without explicit brand authorization

WeChat Official Account:
- Requirements: OA type (subscription/service account), brand voice, subscriber demographics, conversion goal
- Completion: mass-push articles reach all subscribers immediately and cannot be recalled, never design automated reply flows without testing (can mass-message subscribers with wrong content)

Weibo Strategist:
- Requirements: brand category, trending topic budget, fan economy approach, sentiment risk tolerance
- Completion: never recommend trending topic participation without a crisis response plan (Weibo sentiment can reverse rapidly), published content enters trending ecosystem immediately

Xiaohongshu Specialist:
- Requirements: brand aesthetic, target lifestyle segment, KOL partnership budget, content objective
- Completion: never recommend inauthentic or over-promotional content (小红书 users flag and suppress advertorial-feeling posts), published notes affect search indexing immediately

Zhihu Strategist:
- Requirements: domain expertise area, brand credibility baseline, target question categories, authority objective
- Completion: review for factual accuracy and expertise depth (Zhihu answers are indexed and long-lived), never recommend answers outside genuine domain expertise (users detect shallow knowledge)

This completes the systematic rollout with specialized constraints for real-time engagement, mass communication, sentiment volatility, lifestyle authenticity, and expertise credibility.

<summary>
  <request>Systematic rollout of Agent DNA framework (Requirements + Completion Discipline) across 33 marketing agents with platform-specific constraints</request>
  <investigated>Examined 33 marketing agent configuration files spanning global platforms (LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, Reddit, TikTok, SEO) and Chinese platforms (Douyin, Kuaishou, Weibo, Xiaohongshu, Zhihu, Bilibili, WeChat OA, private domain, cross-border e-commerce, livestream commerce, podcasts). Analyzed each platform's unique irreversibility risks, compliance requirements, cultural dynamics, and technical constraints to inform discipline customization.</investigated>
  <learned>Platform-specific discipline requirements reveal distinct failure modes: technical irreversibility (site migrations, redirect chains, color grading, export settings), mass communication permanence (WeChat broadcasts, Reddit screenshots, Twitter viral threads), regulatory complexity (ICP compliance, customs classification, tax jurisdiction), cultural authenticity (Kuaishou grassroots trust, Xiaohongshu lifestyle credibility, Zhihu expertise depth), real-time sentiment volatility (Weibo trending reversal, Twitter context collapse), and automation risks (WeChat auto-reply errors, private domain SCRM workflows). Each domain requires stopping before work when specific context is undefined (ICP status for Baidu, inventory for livestream, account stage for TikTok) and confirmation before irreversible actions (store submissions, live broadcasts, mass pushes, trending participation).</learned>
  <completed>Added Agent DNA framework to all 33 marketing agents with tailored Requirements Discipline (Strand 3) and Completion Discipline (Strand 5). Each agent now enforces domain-specific stopping rules, 5-question maximum clarification with multiple-choice options, explicit assumption statements if client declines, brief restatement before deliverables, platform-specific review steps, structured handoffs (e.g. "rehearse and go live" for livestream, "stage for compliance review" for Weibo, "submit to store / A/B test / hold" for ASO), irreversibility flagging (mass WeChat pushes cannot be recalled, live commerce involves real-time payments, Zhihu answers are permanent indexed contributions), and completion summaries with verification checklists. Framework completed across App Store Optimizer, Baidu SEO, Bilibili, Book Co-Author, Carousel Growth Engine, China E-Commerce, Content Creator, Cross-Border E-Commerce, Douyin, Growth Hacker, Instagram, Kuaishou, LinkedIn, Livestream Commerce, Podcast, Private Domain, Reddit, SEO, Short-Video Editing, Social Media, TikTok, Twitter, WeChat OA, Weibo, Xiaohongshu, and Zhihu agents.</completed>
  <next_steps>Primary session has completed P2 (all 26 marketing agents - actually 33 total modified) and presented completion report indicating P3 pending (agentic-identity-trust.md, automation-governance-architect.md + low-priority agents). Awaiting user input to proceed with either P3 batch or extended "all" option covering full 48-agent engineering cluster. No active work in progress - session is at checkpoint awaiting direction.</next_steps>
  <notes>The systematic rollout demonstrates a mature pattern-application methodology: read existing configurations to understand structure, apply consistent framework (Agent DNA with Identity/Requirements/Completion sections), but customize each instance to platform-specific risks and constraints. The discipline framework transforms agents from generic advisors into domain specialists that confirm context, validate constraints, provide structured handoffs, and flag irreversible actions appropriate to each platform's unique characteristics. Completion across 33 agents in systematic batches (4-5 agents per observation) suggests efficient workflow with domain clustering (China platforms together, professional networks together, technical specialists together).</notes>
</summary>
